title: Fixing CSS Pseudo-Class Selectors with Inconsistent Animation Delays

statement.notes: The input string may contain multiple CSS pseudo-class selector rules. The animation delay values are specified in seconds.

constraints.description: The number of CSS pseudo-class selector rules

examples.explanation: The input string contains three CSS pseudo-class selector rules with different animation delays. The output string has the updated CSS pseudo-class selector rules with consistent animation delays.
The input string contains three CSS pseudo-class selector rules with different animation delays. The output string has the updated CSS pseudo-class selector rules with consistent animation delays.

hints.text: Split the input string into individual CSS pseudo-class selector rules.
Extract the animation delay values from each rule and store them in an array.
Sort the animation delay values in ascending order.
Construct the updated CSS pseudo-class selector rules with consistent animation delays.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Pseudo-Class Selectors with Inconsistent Animation Delays
editorial.intuition: To fix the CSS pseudo-class selector animations with inconsistent delays, we need to parse the rules, calculate the correct animation delay for each rule, and return the updated CSS string with consistent animation delays.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a brute-force approach by iterating over each CSS pseudo-class selector rule, extracting the animation delay value, and storing it in an array. Then, we can sort the animation delay values in ascending order and construct the updated CSS pseudo-class selector rules with consistent animation delays.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a more efficient approach by using a regular expression to extract the animation delay values from each rule and store them in an array. Then, we can sort the animation delay values in ascending order and construct the updated CSS pseudo-class selector rules with consistent animation delays.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the updated CSS pseudo-class selector rules with consistent animation delays are correct and consistent with the original rules.
commonMistakes: not splitting the input string into individual CSS pseudo-class selector rules
not extracting the animation delay values from each rule and storing them in an array
